data.description:
## Why

Cross-system sync (Jira today; Linear / GitHub Issues planned) must propagate a LOCAL removal of a reference (detach a parent, unlink a dependency) WITHOUT it being resurrected by the next inbound pass. The naive approach — gate outbound DELETE on an 'ever-seen' set projected from raw event history (as `local_label_intent` does for labels) — fails CLOSED across `compact_ticket`: compaction collapses the event log to a SNAPSHOT whose `compiled_state` holds only CURRENT refs, so a removal performed at/after the compaction boundary is re-resurrected (the exact churn). We also found the shipped parent CLEAR (drum-ruler-suit / PR #54) is un-gated and clobbers a human-set-in-Jira parent on a locally-parentless ticket.

The field (Unito/Exalate/FolderSync/Syncthing/ghas-jira-integration) validates the intent-gate concept; the load-bearing risks are state DURABILITY and unbounded growth. This story builds the durable, reusable primitive ONCE so parent + links (and future peers) share one provider-agnostic pattern.

## What

A monotonic, compaction-surviving `managed_refs` provenance set maintained by the reducer in `compiled_state` (so `process_snapshot` restores it for free across compaction), plus a shared, provider-agnostic gate the outbound differ uses to decide REMOVE-vs-ADOPT for any reference kind.

- `managed_refs`: the union of every logical reference this ticket has ever managed — normalized `(kind, target_local_id)` where kind in {parent, blocks, depends_on, relates_to}. Folded in process_create (initial parent + deps), process_link, and parent EDIT (incl. inbound-adopted refs); NEVER removed by UNLINK/detach. Deterministically serialized (sorted) for SNAPSHOT byte-parity.
- Shared gate `should_propagate_removal(ref, managed_refs) -> bool`: a peer ref present + locally absent => propagate DELETE iff ref in managed_refs; else ADOPT inbound. Provider-agnostic (operates on local logical refs; each provider maps to its entity).
- Bounded reclamation hook: prune a managed_ref once it is absent BOTH locally and in the peer snapshot (converged), keeping the set bounded without resurrecting human re-adds.

## Scope

In scope: the reducer field + compaction preservation + byte-parity; the shared gate helper; reclamation; docs establishing the reusable pattern. The parent + link CONSUMERS are tracked as child tickets (parent-hardening bug + wake-inn-parse). Out of scope: actual Linear/GitHub providers (future); GC tuning beyond the converged-prune.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] A reusable, provider-agnostic managed-ref provenance primitive both parent and link removal sync consume, with no Jira-specific logic in the shared gate.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Reducer maintains `compiled_state.managed_refs` as a monotonic, deterministically-sorted union of normalized (kind, target) refs, folded from CREATE/LINK/parent-EDIT (including inbound-adopted), never reduced by UNLINK/detach.
- [ ] `managed_refs` survives `compact_ticket` (restored by process_snapshot) — a removal performed before OR after a compaction boundary still propagates and does NOT resurrect; covered by a test that compacts then reconciles.
- [ ] SNAPSHOT byte-parity is preserved (deterministic serialization; the existing byte-parity guard passes).
- [ ] A shared `should_propagate_removal` gate (provider-agnostic) is consumed by BOTH the parent and link outbound paths.
- [ ] A reclamation path prunes converged refs (absent locally AND in the peer snapshot) so managed_refs stays bounded; covered by a test.
- [ ] docs/ documents the pattern as the template for future peer-sync providers (Linear/GitHub).
data.title: Managed-reference provenance: a compaction-surviving, provider-agnostic removal-sync gate
